summary: Improved matching for lbshadow spline tangent and shadow update code in src/melee/lb/lbshadow.c. The PR body reports lbShadow_8000E9F0 improved to 95.80645% and lbShadow_8000F38C to 99.69586%, verified with configure/ninja and checkdiff on both functions.
what_changed: In lbShadow_8000E9F0, preserved the original spline parameter in a new orig_u local before scaling u by spline->numcv - 1, then used orig_u for the exact 1.0F endpoint check.
In lbShadow_8000E9F0, rewrote the Bezier tangent coefficient expression from -((4.0F * t) - 1.0F) to 1.0F - (4.0F * t), an algebraically equivalent form chosen for codegen.
Added a static inline lbShadow_Sqrtf helper that uses __frsqrte plus three Newton-Raphson refinement steps, volatile f32 storage, static f64 constants, and a local 0x38-byte initialized stack buffer.
Replaced the conditional sqrtf(dist) call in lbShadow_8000F38C with lbShadow_Sqrtf(dist).
Adjusted lbShadow_8000F38C stack padding from PAD_STACK(0x48) to PAD_STACK(0x10).
Hoisted several locals in lbShadow_8000F38C, including nextGx, hasObj, i, and shadow2, from inner scopes to the function-level local declaration area.
Changed a few local assignment patterns in lbShadow_8000F38C, including using a temporary HSD_JObj* jobj before HSD_ShadowAddObject and reusing hoisted shadow2 and i locals.
Reordered assignment to upVec.x and upVec.y while leaving values unchanged.
smart_moves: Preserving orig_u separately from the scaled u lets the code use the unmodified parameter for the spline endpoint special case while still matching the later index/tangent calculations.
The custom lbShadow_Sqrtf avoids library sqrtf codegen differences and reproduces the target style using __frsqrte refinement, which is a common matching tactic on PowerPC/GameCube code.
Hoisting nextGx, hasObj, shadow2, and i out of nested blocks likely helped align stack slot allocation and lifetime with the original binary without changing behavior.
Small algebraic rewrites and assignment reordering were used as low-risk codegen nudges while keeping the math behavior equivalent.
The PR included explicit checkdiff commands for both touched functions, giving concrete evidence that the matching changes targeted lbShadow_8000E9F0 and lbShadow_8000F38C specifically.
decomp_lessons: When a function scales an input parameter for indexing but later needs to compare the original parameter value, introducing an orig_* local can both clarify intent and improve matchability.
For math-heavy decompilation, equivalent C expressions can generate different instruction sequences; expression spelling such as 1.0F - 4.0F * t versus a negated parenthesized expression can matter.

Replacing standard math library calls with local inline helpers using platform intrinsics may be necessary when the original binary used reciprocal-square-root estimate sequences instead of a direct sqrtf call.
Local variable scope can affect stack layout and register allocation; moving variables to outer scope is a valid matching tactic when behavior remains unchanged.
PAD_STACK values should be revisited after variable lifetime and helper-function changes because stack layout improvements can reduce or alter manual padding requirements.
naming_conventions: Used orig_u to name the preserved unscaled copy of parameter u.
Named the local sqrt helper lbShadow_Sqrtf, following the module/function prefix style used by lbShadow symbols.
Used nextGx for a hoisted copy of lgobj->next_gx.
Used hasObj as a boolean-like s32 tracking whether any object was added to a shadow.
Used shadow2 for a second HSD_Shadow* local where the existing shadow local name was unavailable or unsuitable.
Used jobj for a temporary HSD_JObj* cast from gobj->hsd_obj before passing it to HSD_ShadowAddObject.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Stored the original u in orig_u before mutating u, then compared orig_u against 1.0F for the endpoint branch.
Changed algebraic form of Bezier coefficient computation to influence generated floating-point operations.
Implemented sqrt via __frsqrte and repeated Newton-Raphson refinement rather than relying on sqrtf.
Used volatile f32 y in lbShadow_Sqrtf to force a float store/load or rounding behavior consistent with target codegen.
Included static const f64 half and three constants in the sqrt helper, likely to steer constant type and load behavior.
Inserted a zero-initialized local u8 _[0x38] buffer inside lbShadow_Sqrtf, likely as a deliberate stack/codegen matching pad.
Reduced PAD_STACK in lbShadow_8000F38C from 0x48 to 0x10 after other stack-shaping changes.
Hoisted locals from inner scopes to outer scope to adjust lifetime/register allocation.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available beyond the PR body and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that replace sqrtf with __frsqrte-based inline helpers for PowerPC matching.
Find lbshadow PRs that adjust PAD_STACK or hoist locals to improve lbShadow_8000F38C.
Search for uses of orig_u or preserved original parameters in spline interpolation/tangent functions.
Compare later lbShadow_8000E9F0 and lbShadow_8000F38C matching percentages after this PR.
